title: Chapter Ten: Radiant Demon Wing
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Ma Jin, the leader of Radiant Demon Wing, was a man built from scars.

A diagonal wound drew a line from the left side of his face to the right — a wound that should have killed him. His body radiated rough, formidable energy. Not refined. Not elegant. Hard. Like a blade that had been used too often and never properly cleaned.

He stood at the centre of his division's training ground and looked at the two new warriors.

"You. Lightning Flash."

"Aye."

An ordinary answer. Yeon-shin's face did not change. The sharp gaze in Ma Jin's fierce eyes held powerful inner energy, but Yeon-shin had spoken his mind to the Desolate Sect Leader herself. A scarred division chief did not intimidate him.

Ma Jin studied his unwavering expression and nodded.

"You have spirit. Men like you don't die easily."

The training ground was vast — wide enough for five hundred, with various weapons racked along the far wall. Around them, thirty men and women of the Radiant Demon Wing sat or stood, watching the newcomers with the measured eyes of veterans assessing fresh meat.

"The martial divisions under the Divine Sword Corps are like a clan of the Central Plains. You will learn martial arts and carry out missions here. Everyone is your senior and your kin."

"Lord Ma, I have a question!"

Won-chang. Excitement had overcome his anxiety. He looked beside himself.

"Speak."

"Does learning martial arts here mean everyone shares the same techniques?"

"Secret family techniques are yours alone. What's taught are only Radiant Demon Wing's own arts. The rest is not obligatory."

"The famous Radiant Demon Formation!" Won-chang's face was rapturous. The golden dragon on his headband seemed to flutter in sympathy.

*Radiant Demon Formation.*

Even Yeon-shin knew that name. Radiant Demon Wing was famous for clashing with the twenty-four Plum Blossom Swordsmen of Mount Hua without suffering a single casualty — an event that had added strength to those who called Desolate Sect the greatest in the world.

"You'll train hard if you want to survive," Ma Jin said. His scarred face was serious. "Although the Desolate Sect is an imperial sect, attacking a warrior of the Desolate Sect is not considered treason. That's why you must become stronger. Endlessly. If you can't, you die."

"Why isn't it treason?" Yeon-shin asked.

Before Ma Jin could answer, Won-chang spoke first. "From the imperial court's perspective, the crime of treason must be detected and the entire clan exterminated. If they don't do that, the empire's dignity suffers. But there are many eccentric people in the martial world — people who have lost their minds practising demonic arts, or whose lightness skills are so extraordinary they can touch officials and still escape to the deep mountains. They can't grant imperial dignity to Desolate Sect warriors, whose very mission is to clash with such people."

"Crazy bastard," Ma Jin said. But he smirked and did not stop the conversation.

*This Heon Won-chang. Unlike his usual frivolous self, he shows flashes of intelligence. Usually when talking about power structures.*

A man sat among the warriors — a handsome figure with a blue headband, lounging with casual disregard. Distinct features. Gentle impression. Free-spirited energy. The tail of his headband fluttered in a breeze that seemed to follow him alone.

Extraordinary at first glance.

Ma Jin pointed at him. "That's the one who came back after getting stabbed by Namgung's First Sword."

"That Emperor Sword Form was terrifyingly strong," the man said, waving a hand.

*Emperor Sword Form — the peerless art that only the legitimate successor of the Namgung family can learn. And Namgung's First Sword is considered one of the strongest masters in South Zhili. Yet this man returned alive.*

The casual posture was deceptive. Looking closer, Yeon-shin could see the energy waves around his body — thoroughly controlled, revealing nothing. The very fact of his survival was proof of his skill.

The man stood abruptly. Ma Jin stepped back, as if this had been arranged.

"Well then — shall we start with Lightning Flash?" He walked toward Yeon-shin, his gaze drawn to the sword at his waist. He grinned. "If you came here at that age, you must have talent. Let me see your sword aptitude. I'm good at checking that sort of thing."

"You're good at seeing talent?"

"Yeah. I'm good at feeling the wind. Even a child's basic form has sword wind. You can read how they wield the blade — with what feeling. There's a friend here named Baek Miryeo whose sword is overflowing with talent. Very intuitive. You should train with her."

"Let's do it."

"I'm called Cheong Myeong. Born and raised in Xiangyang."

"Jeong Yeon-shin. Xinye."

"So there are places like that too."

Cheong Myeong laughed. Yeon-shin's hand went to the Desolate Sword. Black-bladed, balanced, high-grade — incomparable to the iron sword he had carried from home. When he circulated his energy, the blade trembled with the familiar resonance of sword-body unity.

*Rumour says there's a forge of dwarves here. If true, it explains the quality.*

Before Cheong Myeong drew, Yeon-shin noted the armour-like calluses on both his hands. The lean, firm muscles visible through his sleeves. The upright posture that attested to lofty martial power.

Cheong Myeong's gaze approached overwhelmingly, as if asking: *When have I ever been idle?*

*Nothing to worry about. This is a place to showcase skill.*

He emptied his mind. Left only Cheong Myeong's breathing in his senses. Inhale. Very slow exhale.

*Now.*

He reversed the energy flow — the force that should have gone to his left foot surged the opposite way. A thud. The training ground floor cracked under his right foot. The Fate Conquering Scripture launched him forward.

In a single leap, Cheong Myeong's upper body was close enough to touch. The Desolate Sword cleared the scabbard. The five-pace distance shattered, and a streak of sword light cut through the morning air. Contracted muscles and layered energy exploded outward — the full power of the swift sword.

KWANG.

The collision nearly tore the sword from his grip. His vision went white. His body flew backward.

*Better than I thought.*

He concentrated energy on three acupoints around his right knee — Yanggu, Xuehai, Xiyangguan. Calf muscles wriggled like a predator's haunches. His right foot punched into the floor, sinking deep. He used the anchor. Spun. His body pivoted half a turn, the Desolate Sword drawing a screaming semicircle aimed at the trapezius behind Cheong Myeong's neck.

A sound like tearing silk as the blade cut the air. Then Cheong Myeong's whole body pulsed, seemed to blur, and his sword was suddenly, impossibly close.

White.

CHWAAAK.

*Twisting Silk Force, rotated properly.*

He had not seen it. He did not need to. The sword buzzing in his grip sensed the texture of the air on his behalf. Cheong Myeong's words about feeling the wind — the inspiration was developing the realm of sword-body unity in this very moment.

JJEONG.

The clash rang like striking a bell. True energy scattered in all directions, tearing his clothes at the seams. His arm was thrown upward. He was surprised he still held the sword.

He threw himself backward — Iron Plate Bridge, his right foot still anchored. A white blade whispered past the tip of his nose. Hair fell — severed before it could fully drift down. The sight was eerie.

Yeon-shin kicked. His body somersaulted. His foot caught something at the apex —

Cheong Myeong's headband.

It came free. Pointed ears. Golden hair.

Elf.

Features no human could possess. Yeon-shin, who had seen many Elves recently, registered it with barely a thought and landed in a crouch. He adjusted his grip and stared at Cheong Myeong with transparent eyes.

*Next — applying Fate Conquering Scripture to the Desolate Sword —*

He stopped.

The hall was silent. Every warrior in Radiant Demon Wing was staring at him. Cheong Myeong had gone still, his expression stunned.

The spar was over. The atmosphere would not permit another exchange.

Yeon-shin sheathed his sword carefully and asked with genuine curiosity:

"How was I? You said you would assess my talent."

Silence.

"I know you went easy. Tell me honestly."

Not only the masters who had faced the Plum Blossom Swordsmen, but even Cheong Myeong — who had crossed swords with Namgung's First Sword — seemed to have forgotten how to speak. They just stared.

Only after a long moment did Ma Jin open his mouth, contorting his scar.

"You. How old did you say you were?"

"Fifteen. But does that matter? I won't be lenient because of my age. Only my current skill matters."

No one heard the second sentence. Starting with Cheong Myeong — who threw his sword clattering to the floor — the warriors began sprawling and muttering, one by one, as if the tension in the hall had been a physical thing and it had just been cut.

"That's a monstrosity."

"A mistake of the heavens."

"I've been to the Gathering of Talents. Never seen anything like that. Even if you learn the Path of Sensation from birth."

"I was the supreme prodigy in our city."

"Supreme my arse."

Ma Jin did not bother quieting them. He gestured to Won-chang.

"Next. Heon Won-chang. You said you came from Shaanxi."

"M-me? Right now?"

"Stand in position. I'll assess you."

No one paid close attention. Only Yeon-shin watched — scanning each exchange for anything to learn and take away. Won-chang fought hard. His swordsmanship was sound, his energy honest. He lost. But he lost on his feet.

---

"Radiant Demon Formation is a martial art that does not discriminate between weapons."

Ma Jin began teaching the next day. He had just returned from a long mission and was guaranteed a month of rest — rest he chose to spend on the two newest members.

"Swords, sabres, spears, fists, palms. Hands, feet, axes, sticks, whips. If you master it properly, it encompasses all."

"How is that possible?" Won-chang asked. His eyelids were swollen blue from the previous day.

"The formation is about how to use the body. A contemplation of everything that can be done with human hands and feet. It's shallow compared to a sword sect focused solely on one weapon. But we are not Wudang priests swinging a single ancient sword for transcendence."

His eyes found Yeon-shin.

"Our martial arts are always fierce battles. Even solo missions are premised on chaotic fights. Fighting is a collection of variables. Would you insist on using a sword if you lost your footing? Master the Radiant Demon Formation, and you won't hesitate to throw away your blade. The level of choice is different. It often lets you survive."

*He is thinking of the kick that took Cheong Myeong's headband.*

On the contrary, Yeon-shin recalled Cheong Myeong himself — who had thrown his sword down after the spar ended. Not in frustration. In demonstration. The master of Radiant Demon Formation did not need a sword to be dangerous.

"The five meanings of the combat route."

Ma Jin walked to a brick tower at the edge of the ground. He settled his energy — let a powerful aura flare, then stripped it away completely, showing that he held nothing back. He stretched out his bare fist and struck.

Thud.

The brick tower collapsed with a crash.

"The optimal moment and the best strike. The combat route is the trajectory that draws it. If you follow the perfect route, the ultimate strike comes out. Body techniques are about seeing the opponent's attacks. But the combat route starts from the realm of instinct. You don't even need to see your own attacks."

He turned to Yeon-shin without dusting off his hand.

"This is the realm of mastery. You can't achieve it from seeing it once. The more you train, the more instinctively you find the optimal route. Take it as a lifelong task."

"Is that so?"

*It doesn't seem so.*

Yeon-shin was looking at the rubble. The fist empty of energy. The strike. The collapse.

*The body at the right time. The right place. The right force.*

But it went further. Ma Jin had shown one direction. One path. One optimal route to a single target. But what if the route was not a line? What if it was a web — branching, splitting, converging — and the body could choose between ten paths in the space of a single breath?

The swift sword was speed. The Radiant Demon Formation was adaptability. The Fate Conquering Scripture was control over the body's energy. What if he wove them together?

Something ignited behind his eyes. Not a thought. An architecture. A framework of motion that encompassed everything he had learned — the Dynamic Technique, the swift sword, the Scripture, and now the combat route — merged into something that had no name yet.

*I think I can do this differently. Right now, even —*

A sky-blue light flickered in Yeon-shin's eyes, bright as lightning through storm cloud.

His hand rose.
